title: modes::Modifier::Debug variant overlap with std::fmt::Debug + Display tested to match but Display omits modifier markers
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/modes/mod.rs:402-404 (display_name) and 372-377 (matching_preset linear scan)
## Violation
display_name returns the preset name (`"create"`) for a matching mode but the AXIS STRING (`"surgical/architect/unrestricted"`) for a custom one — and never includes the modifiers in either case. `matching_preset()` performs a linear scan of all 8 presets per call (with full BehaviorMode equality compares including the modifier Vec) to determine which to return. This is called every time anything wants a human label.

Hot-path callers that render the mode for status (TUI/CLI) pay this scan on every status redraw. For an idle session that's wasted work; for an active session it's also wasted work because the result almost never changes.
## Mandated refactor
Cache matching_preset on the BehaviorMode struct as a private OnceLock<Option<Preset>>. Or — simpler — pre-compute a `const PRESET_TABLE: &[(BehaviorMode, Preset)]` and binary-search by axis triple. Have display_name include modifiers when present (`"create [bold, debug]"`) so the rendered label round-trips with the actual configuration.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
